initiatives.name: BeLoved Asheville
initiatives.description: A grassroots organization building community power through mutual aid, housing justice, and economic dignity. BeLoved works to create systemic change while providing direct support through their street medic team, community house, food distribution, and advocacy for unhoused neighbors.

initiatives.name: AVL Survival Program
initiatives.description: A mutual aid collective fostering community care and direct support through survival programs in Asheville. They coordinate resource distribution, solidarity networks, and community defense while building power through neighborhood organizing and creating sustainable systems of collective care.

initiatives.name: Pansy Collective
initiatives.description: A queer mutual aid collective in Asheville providing direct support, community care, and solidarity to LGBTQ+ community members through resource sharing, emergency assistance, and collective organizing.

initiatives.name: Tranzmission Prison Project
initiatives.description: A grassroots organization providing mutual aid and support for incarcerated LGBTQ+ individuals. They coordinate book and resource distribution, pen pal programs, and solidarity networks while building power through prison abolition organizing and creating pathways for support and connection for incarcerated LGBTQ+ people.

initiatives.name: Firestorm
initiatives.description: A worker-owned cooperative and radical bookstore fostering mutual aid through accessible literature, community events, and political education. They create spaces for learning, organizing, and building solidarity while providing resources for social movements and community empowerment.

initiatives.name: The Steady Collective
initiatives.description: The Steady Collective distributes Naloxone, and provides syringe exchange services, wound care and a range of other services in three locations around Asheville.

initiatives.name: Blue Ridge Anarchist Black Cross
initiatives.description: We are prison abolitionists. We believe incarceration is not a solution to societal problems and support those challenging institutional oppression.
